title: merremia digitata var. cinerea
intro: Merremia digitata var. cinerea, also known as the climbing passionflower, is a perennial climber plant species belonging to the family Convolvulaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment, and can be used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
